summary: Reproduce Pandya Conv_plot: self-convergence of the conformal-BDNK Gaussian clump. Built the reference C code at RES_MULTIPLE=1,2,4 (N=129,257,513) and ran the Julia engine at matching resolutions (same final t=318.75). Using N=513 as reference, dx-weighted L1 errors of N=129/257: Julia err 0.2902/0.1124 вҶ’ order p=1.37; C code err 0.2903/0.1143 вҶ’ order p=1.34. The two codes' error curves are coincident and the orders match to 0.03. Order ~1.35 (between p=1 and p=2) reflects the under-resolved narrow clump (width 5 on dx=3.125), not the formal Heun+WENO5 order вҖ” both codes show the same reduced order, confirming the same scheme.
evidence: figures/conf_convergence.png VLM вҖ” Julia (p=1.37) and C (p=1.34) error curves coincident

summary: Complete the conformal-BDNK code-validation triad: STEP/Riemann ID (ОөL=1, ОөR=0.1, u=0) vs reference C code. Added init_step to the engine matching the C code's sharp step. The discontinuity resolves into a self-similar Riemann fan (leftward rarefaction + middle state ОөвүҲ0.31 + rightward front); Julia vs C agree to MACHINE LEVEL: t=0 dev 1.4e-16, evolution dev вүӨ4.7e-7, overall max rel-dev 4.7e-5%. Tighter than Gaussian (0.06%) and shock (0.9%) because the fan's features are well-resolved (vs the under-resolved narrow Gaussian peak). All THREE reference-code ID types now reproduced.
evidence: figures/conf_step_overlay.png VLM вҖ” C dots on Julia lines across the full fan; max rel-dev 4.7e-5%
